skill.name: error-debugging-multi-agent-review
skill.description: Guides structured multi-agent code review workflows for debugging, security, architecture, performance, and quality analysis.
skill.summary: Plan and synthesize code reviews across specialized AI agents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found a single Markdown-only skill with illustrative Python snippets and ordinary text, with no executable scripts, network access, credential access, or prompt injection attempts. The residual risk is low because the skill asks agents to inspect arbitrary code paths supplied by users.

low_findings.title: False positive external command indicators
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown inline code, a template variable, and fenced Python examples. No evidence found of Ruby shell backtick execution or runnable command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown documentation and the matched regions are examples or inline formatting. There are no scripts, command runners, or executable Ruby constructs in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False positive weak cryptography indicators
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain ordinary descriptive words and workflow labels, not cryptographic algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA1 usage, or any cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are prose or example phase names. The skill contains no cryptographic code or recommendations.

low_findings.title: False positive reconnaissance indicators
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe review execution and hybrid orchestration strategy. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, or system reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is conceptual documentation for agent workflow planning. It does not request system information or instruct execution of reconnaissance utilities.

content.value_statement: Complex debugging and review work often needs security, architecture, performance, and quality perspectives. This skill provides a structured workflow for routing review tasks to specialized Claude, Codex, and Claude Code agents.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to use multi-agent review for code debugging workflows.
Outlines agent types for security, architecture, performance, compliance, and quality review.
Provides routing concepts for selecting specialized agents from code context.
Describes parallel, sequential, and hybrid review execution patterns.
Explains synthesis, conflict resolution, and quality validation for review results.
limitations: Provides conceptual guidance, not a runnable orchestration framework.
Does not include working agent implementations or integration code.
Depends on the host assistant to inspect files and coordinate agents safely.
Requires user-supplied project context for useful review recommendations.

use_cases.title: Debug a complex regression
use_cases.description: Coordinate code quality, architecture, and test coverage reviews to isolate likely causes.

use_cases.title: Review a risky pull request
use_cases.description: Split a pull request across security, performance, and maintainability perspectives before merge.

use_cases.title: Assess release readiness
use_cases.description: Use sequential review phases for design, implementation, testing, and deployment concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a clear review structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the multi-agent review workflow to create a review plan for this project. Identify the agent roles, review order, and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Debugging Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Find likely causes for a reported defect.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this bug report and code context. Route the work across relevant agents and summarize the most likely failure areas.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate a change from multiple engineering perspectives.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a multi-agent review of this pull request. Cover security, architecture, performance, test coverage, and maintainability. Prioritize findings by impact.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Review Synthesis
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine conflicting review perspectives into a decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Orchestrate a hybrid multi-agent review for this release candidate. Resolve conflicts between agent findings and produce a final readiness recommendation.

output_examples.input: A user asks for review coverage on a web application change.
output_examples.output: The assistant assigns security, architecture, performance, and quality roles, then returns a prioritized review plan with expected evidence for each role.

output_examples.input: A user provides a defect report and related files.
output: The assistant separates likely root causes by domain.
It lists which agent should inspect each area.
It ends with verification steps for the suspected fix.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether a release candidate is ready.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes design, implementation, testing, and deployment readiness findings, then states the remaining blockers and recommended next action.

best_practices: Define the review goal and risk areas before assigning agent roles.
Keep agent findings tied to concrete files, behavior, or tests.
Synthesize disagreements into one prioritized action list.
anti_patterns: Do not treat conceptual agent examples as working automation.
Do not run broad reviews without a clear target and success criteria.
Do not merge agent findings without resolving conflicting recommendations.

faq.question: Does this skill run multiple agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides orchestration guidance for an assistant that can coordinate specialized review work.

faq.question: Can it review security issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes security auditor roles and recommends security review as part of broader code assessment.

faq.question: Does it include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. The Python snippets are illustrative examples in Markdown documentation.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Use a focused project path, pull request, defect report, or code snippet with clear review goals.

faq.question: Is this suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when they inspect codebases.

faq.question: How should results be delivered?
faq.answer: Results should be prioritized, evidence-based, and grouped by impact, owner, and verification step.
